summary: A small recipe frontend application demonstrating how to consume Conjure-generated TypeScript API bindings in a React+Redux app. It showcases the end-to-end pattern: a Conjure API definition (defined externally in conjure-java-example) is code-generated into TypeScript types and service clients, which are then consumed by a React frontend via conjure-client's DefaultHttpApiBridge. The app implements a RecipeBook UI with CRUD operations against a Conjure-defined RecipeBookService.

ontologyConcepts.name: IRecipe
ontologyConcepts.description: A Conjure-generated TypeScript interface representing a recipe object with a name and an ordered list of recipe steps. This is the primary domain entity in the example.

ontologyConcepts.name: IRecipeStep
ontologyConcepts.description: A Conjure-generated discriminated union type representing a single step in a recipe. Uses a 'type' discriminator field with variants: 'mix', 'chop', and 'bake'. Each variant carries different payload data. This demonstrates Conjure's union type code generation pattern.

ontologyConcepts.name: IRecipeStep_Mix
ontologyConcepts.description: The 'mix' variant of IRecipeStep. Contains a 'mix' field which is an array of ingredient name strings.

ontologyConcepts.name: IRecipeStep_Chop
ontologyConcepts.description: The 'chop' variant of IRecipeStep. Contains a 'chop' field which is a single ingredient name string.

ontologyConcepts.name: IRecipeStep_Bake
ontologyConcepts.description: The 'bake' variant of IRecipeStep. Contains a 'bake' field with nested temperature (degree + unit) and durationInSeconds properties.

ontologyConcepts.name: TemperatureUnit
ontologyConcepts.description: A Conjure-generated enum with values FAHRENHEIT and CELCIUS, used in bake step temperature specifications.

ontologyConcepts.name: RecipeBookService
ontologyConcepts.description: A Conjure-generated service client class that provides typed methods for interacting with the RecipeBook API. Instantiated with a DefaultHttpApiBridge and exposes methods like getAllRecipes(). This is the primary integration point between the frontend and the Conjure-defined backend API.

apiSurface.name: DefaultHttpApiBridge
apiSurface.description: The HTTP bridge/transport layer from conjure-typescript-runtime. Configured with a token, userAgent, and baseUrl. Passed to Conjure-generated service classes to enable HTTP communication with the backend. This is the core runtime component that translates Conjure service calls into HTTP requests.
apiSurface.usage: new DefaultHttpApiBridge({ token, userAgent: { productName, productVersion }, baseUrl })

apiSurface.name: RecipeBookService
apiSurface.description: Generated service client. Constructed with an IHttpApiBridge instance. Provides typed async methods (returning Promises) for each endpoint defined in the Conjure API spec.

methods.name: getAllRecipes
methods.description: Fetches all recipes from the backend service.

architecturePatterns.name: Conjure Service Client Instantiation
architecturePatterns.description: Generated Conjure service classes are instantiated by passing a configured DefaultHttpApiBridge. The bridge handles HTTP transport concerns (authentication, serialization, base URL routing) while the generated client provides the typed API surface. This decouples transport from API shape.
architecturePatterns.example: const bridge = new DefaultHttpApiBridge({ token, userAgent, baseUrl }); const service = new RecipeBookService(bridge);

architecturePatterns.name: Conjure Union Type Discrimination
architecturePatterns.description: Conjure-generated union types use a 'type' string discriminator field with TypeScript discriminated union interfaces (IRecipeStep_Mix, IRecipeStep_Chop, IRecipeStep_Bake). Consumer code switches on step.type to narrow the type and access variant-specific fields. Each variant interface has its own specific payload shape.
architecturePatterns.example: switch (step.type) { case 'mix': return step.mix; case 'chop': return step.chop; case 'bake': return step.bake; }

architecturePatterns.name: Service Injection via Props
architecturePatterns.description: Rather than using React context or a service locator, Conjure service instances are created at the app root and passed down through React component props. The Redux store handles domain state while services handle API communication as a separate concern.

architecturePatterns.name: Redux + Redoodle Typed Actions
architecturePatterns.description: The app uses Palantir's 'redoodle' library for strongly-typed Redux actions and reducers. TypedAction.define creates type-safe action creators, and TypedReducer.builder creates reducers with handler type inference. combineReducers and reduceCompoundActions compose the root reducer.

architecturePatterns.name: Conjure Types as State
architecturePatterns.description: Conjure-generated interfaces (IRecipe, IRecipeStep) are used directly as Redux state shapes and component state. The component state for CreateRecipeDialog is typed as ICreateRecipeDialogState = IRecipe, showing that Conjure types serve as the domain model throughout the frontend.

architecturePatterns.name: NPM Package Distribution for Generated APIs
architecturePatterns.description: Conjure-generated TypeScript APIs are published as NPM packages (conjure-recipe-example-api) via gradle-conjure's publishTypeScript task. Consumers add them as regular package.json dependencies alongside conjure-client. This enables versioned, shareable API contracts across frontend projects.

typeDefinitions.name: IServices
typeDefinitions.description: Application-level service container holding instantiated Conjure service clients.

typeDefinitions.name: IRecipesAppState
typeDefinitions.definition: interface IRecipesAppState { recipesData: IRecipesDataState; interfaceData: IInterfaceData }
typeDefinitions.description: Root Redux state shape combining domain data (recipes) with UI state (dialog open/close).

typeDefinitions.name: IRecipesDataState
typeDefinitions.description: Redux state slice holding the list of recipes fetched from the Conjure RecipeBookService.

typeDefinitions.name: IInterfaceData
typeDefinitions.description: Redux state slice for UI-level state such as dialog visibility.

typeDefinitions.name: IRecipesAppStateProps
typeDefinitions.definition: interface IRecipesAppStateProps { isDialogueOpen: boolean; recipes: IRecipe[]; services: IServices }
typeDefinitions.description: Props mapped from Redux state for the main RecipesApp component.

typeDefinitions.name: IRecipesAppDispatchProps
typeDefinitions.definition: interface IRecipesAppDispatchProps { addRecipes: (recipe: IRecipe[]) => void; setIsDialogOpen: (isDialogOpen: boolean) => void }
typeDefinitions.description: Props mapped from Redux dispatch for the main RecipesApp component.

conjureRuntime.description: conjure-typescript-runtime - provides DefaultHttpApiBridge for making HTTP calls to Conjure-defined services

conjureGeneratedApi.description: Conjure-generated TypeScript types and service clients for the RecipeBook API, published to NPM by gradle-conjure from conjure-java-example

ui.description: Palantir's Blueprint UI component library for React

stateManagement.description: Redux for state management with Palantir's redoodle library for type-safe actions and reducers
